fact: Working subject is a 1968-era GT40 Mk I style vehicle with Gurney-Weslake Ford 302 configuration.
applicability: This project only; not a verified chassis specification.
label: assumption
notes: User-provided scope.

fact: Chassis number is required before final settings are considered authoritative.
label: verified
notes: Configuration-control rule derived from vehicle variability and safety.

fact: Gurney-Weslake 302 claims require block, head, induction, compression, cam, and build-sheet verification.
label: open
notes: Current web results are leads only.

fact: Secondary sources identify GT40P/1075 as the 1968 Le Mans-winning Mk I.
label: lead_only
notes: Needs primary race records or chassis register confirmation.

fact: Secondary sources describe the 1968 context as 3.0 L prototype limit with up-to-5.0 L homologated Group 4 sports cars.
label: lead_only
notes: Needs FIA Appendix J / homologation primary source.

fact: Mk I transaxle identity must be verified before ratios, oil, preload, or LSD setup are advised.
label: verified
notes: Configuration-control rule.

fact: 1968 FIA Appendix J classified Group 4 as sportscars within recognized production cars, with a 50-car recognition threshold.
label: verified
notes: Regulation fact; still requires the GT40 homologation form for chassis-specific details.

fact: 1968 FIA Appendix J cylinder-capacity classes included over 3,000 cc to 5,000 cc and over 5,000 cc classes.
applicability: Displacement-class context for a 302 cu in / about 4.9 L engine.
label: verified
notes: Regulation fact, not an engine build specification.

fact: 1968 Appendix J sportscars had to have at least two seats.
label: verified
notes: Regulation fact.

fact: Recognition forms could be required at scrutineering or at the start, and competitors had to obtain them through the relevant national sporting authority.
label: verified
notes: Use this as the basis for locating the GT40 recognition form.

fact: For 1968 Group 4 recognition, engine identity included items such as cylinder head, cylinder block, cylinder count, bore, stroke, and crankshaft bearings.
label: verified
notes: Makes Gurney-Weslake head identity and measured bore/stroke primary evidence needs.

fact: For 1968 Group 4 recognition, gearbox identity and recognized gear series were controlled while gearbox and final-drive ratios had broad freedom.
label: verified
notes: Actual transaxle still must be identified before shop settings.

fact: Sportscar brake systems were expected to act on four wheels and retain braking on at least two wheels after leakage or failure in part of the transmission system.
applicability: Brake system safety and period regulation context.
label: verified
notes: Does not provide GT40-specific hydraulic layout or brake-bias settings.

fact: 1968 Group 4 recognition treated suspension through operating principle and component function rather than generic visual similarity.
label: verified
notes: Use to avoid substituting unverified replica or later geometry.

fact: Sportscar weight had to comply with the recognition form and applicable minimum-weight table; weight reduction below the recognized minimum was not permitted.
label: verified
notes: The GT40 recognition form is still needed for the exact value.

fact: FIA Historic Database guidance says event HTP use requires an ASN-certified copy of the original or retrospective homologation form, while website forms are consultation-only.
applicability: Homologation document control and event preparation.
label: verified
notes: Use research copies for mechanics research, but obtain certified copies for event use.

fact: Secondary sources identify GT40P/1075 as the Mk I associated with the 1968 and 1969 Le Mans wins.
applicability: Historical context and source-acquisition lead only.
label: lead_only
notes: Needs ACO, JWA, Ford, chassis-register, or specialist book confirmation before promotion.

fact: Secondary sources list the 1968 Le Mans-winning car as number 9, JW Automotive Engineering, Pedro Rodriguez and Lucien Bianchi, Ford GT40, Ford 4.9 L V8, 331 laps.
label: lead_only
notes: Reportedly derived from an ACO-licensed book, but the book/ACO record has not been inspected directly.

fact: Secondary sources describe the GT40 302 as a Ford small-block package with heavy-duty four-bolt main bearing caps, pressed-in core plugs, and Gurney-Weslake aluminum heads.
label: lead_only
notes: Requires inspection and Ford/FAV/JWA/Gurney-Weslake documentation before mechanic use.

fact: Secondary sources state Weslake provided Gurney-Weslake cylinder heads for Gulf-Wyer GT40 Mk I Le Mans-winning engines in 1968 and 1969.
applicability: Engine history and head-identification lead only.
label: lead_only
notes: Does not verify the installed heads on this car.

fact: Secondary sources connect the 1968 GT40 race context to a 4.9 L / 4942 cc / 302 cu in Ford V8.
label: lead_only
notes: S009 verifies the relevant FIA displacement class, not the exact GT40 engine build.

fact: Secondary sources commonly describe 1968 4.9 L GT40 packages with Weber carburetion.
label: lead_only
notes: Installed carburetors, jetting, fuel pressure, and linkage remain unverified.

fact: Secondary sources commonly associate GT40 Mk I and Mk III cars with a ZF 5-speed transaxle family.
label: lead_only
notes: Installed transaxle must be identified before ratios, oil, preload, or adjustment guidance.

fact: For 1968 Group 4 sportscar recognition, ignition, induction, exhaust, carburettor, filters, and manifolds were treated as free items relative to the controlled engine identity points.
label: verified
notes: Does not provide safe ignition, carburetor, exhaust, or fuel settings.

fact: 1968 Appendix J permitted Group 4 sportscar engine re-boring up to 1.2 mm regardless of engine type.
label: verified
notes: Does not establish the installed engine's bore, wall thickness, piston spec, or rebuild limit.

fact: 1968 Appendix J listed 700 kg as the minimum weight table value for sportscars over 3,000 cc and up to 5,000 cc, while also requiring compliance with the recognition form.
applicability: Regulation context for 302 / about 4.9 L sportscar class.
label: verified
notes: The Ford GT40 recognition form may control the exact applicable weight.

fact: 1968 Appendix J two-seater racing-car provisions required a mounted dry-chemical fire extinguisher of at least 1 kg, roll-over protection, quick-release safety belt, adequate firewall/floor protection, and isolation of fuel/oil/water tanks from the cockpit.
applicability: Period safety benchmark; not automatically a Group 4 GT40 homologation requirement.
label: verified
notes: Use as historical safety context only; current event safety rules may be stricter.

fact: 1968 Appendix J racing-car provisions required a general electric circuit breaker for fire protection and fully enclosed batteries.
applicability: Period safety benchmark; not automatically a Group 4 GT40 homologation requirement.
label: verified
notes: Useful inspection prompt for electrical safety but not a wiring diagram.

fact: 1968 Appendix J racing-car provisions required fuel-tank venting to prevent fumes entering driver or engine compartments and tank isolation so leakage or spillage would not enter cockpit, engine compartment, or exhaust areas.
applicability: Period safety benchmark; not automatically a Group 4 GT40 homologation requirement.
label: verified
notes: Useful inspection prompt; installed fuel-cell/tank specification remains unknown.

fact: 1968 Appendix J racing-car provisions required a catch tank or device to collect oil spilled from the engine or transmission, with capacity requirements stated for formula/free cars.
label: verified
notes: Do not infer GT40 oil-system capacity or breather layout from this.

fact: The FIA Historic Database identifies Ford GT 40 as Recognition/Homologation No. 224 in Group 4.

label: verified

fact: The FIA Ford GT 40 car page lists Group 4, 4736 cc, RWD, 8 cylinders, United Kingdom, and homologation date February 2, 1966.

fact: The Ford GT 40 No. 224 base form records 50 identical cars reached by December 31, 1965.

fact: The Ford GT 40 No. 224 base form records 95 in / 2413 mm wheelbase and 55 in / 1397 mm front and rear track.

fact: The Ford GT 40 No. 224 base form records weight as 905 kg / 1993 lb without fuel or repair tools under the form's weight definition.

fact: The Ford GT 40 No. 224 base form records partly unitized steel chassis/body structure, steel unitary material, fiberglass and steel coachwork, fiberglass bonnet, and fiberglass boot lid.

fact: The Ford GT 40 No. 224 base engine entry records V8, 4-stroke, 101.6 mm bore, 72.9 mm stroke, and 4736 cc / 289 cu in total capacity.

fact: The FIA Historic Database lists Appendix K 2025 as updated on January 1, 2026, and the archived PDF states application from January 1, 2025 and updated on January 1, 2026.
label: verified
notes: Re-check the FIA Appendix K page and event regulations before competition use.

fact: Appendix K requires historic competition cars to respect period specification while applying defined additional safety provisions, and it requires competitors to account for corrosion and ageing of components.
applicability: Current historic safety and originality context.
label: verified
notes: Use as an inspection gate; do not treat period appearance as proof of safe condition.

fact: Appendix K requires battery terminal short-circuit protection, prohibits lithium-based batteries, and requires cockpit batteries to be dry type, secure, and covered with an insulated leakproof cover.
label: verified
notes: Installed battery chemistry, location, hold-down, cover, cable routing, and isolation remain unverified.

fact: Appendix K requires a spark-proof general circuit breaker that cuts electrical circuits except fire-extinguisher circuits and stops the engine, with inside and outside accessibility requirements.
label: verified
notes: Installed master-switch type, location, markings, alternator/generator shutdown behavior, and fire-system exception remain unverified.

fact: Appendix K requires fuel and oil lines and brake cables to be protected externally against deterioration risk and internally against fire risk.
label: verified
notes: Installed line material, routing, bulkhead penetrations, heat shielding, clips, and age remain unverified.

fact: For Period F to K cars, Appendix K sets a fuel-tank safety-foam or D-Stop anti-explosion foil minimum unless period rules required a higher safety standard, and it says period or homologation maximum fuel capacity must not be exceeded.
label: verified
notes: Installed tank/cell type, foam condition, capacity, certification date, and HTP period classification remain unverified.

fact: Appendix K requires protruding quick-release Monza-type fuel caps to have a secondary opening-prevention device and requires non-metallic pressurized fuel lines to be fire/fuel resistant, with 100 psi minimum burst pressure for non-injected cars.
applicability: Current historic fuel-filler and line safety context.
label: verified
notes: Installed filler caps, necks, check valves, fuel-line material, fittings, and pressure rating remain unverified.

fact: Appendix K requires fireproof bulkheads from Period F onward and at least one hand-operated fire extinguisher complying with current Appendix J Article 253-7.3 for cars in all competitions.
label: verified
notes: Installed bulkheads, extinguisher type/date/capacity/mounting, and event fire-system requirements remain unverified.

fact: Appendix K requires oil catch tanks outside rallies: 2 litres minimum up to 2 litres engine capacity and 3 litres minimum above 2 litres, unless original closed-circuit breathing is retained.
label: verified
notes: Installed breather/catch system and retained closed-breathing status remain unverified.

fact: Appendix K requires Period F-onward two-seat racing cars to use FIA 8853/98 or 8853-2016 harnesses; belts must not anchor to seats or seat supports and must be replaced after severe collision, webbing damage/degradation, metal/buckle deformation/rust, or imperfect function.
label: verified
notes: Installed belt standard, age/date, routing, anchorages, seat interaction, and condition remain unverified.

fact: Appendix K requires a rear-facing red warning light at race start, mounted near the vehicle centreline or as an allowed pair on full-width bodywork, driver-switchable, securely attached, and at least 21 W incandescent or equivalent LED output.
label: verified
notes: Installed rear warning light location, output, switch, wiring, and visibility remain unverified.

fact: S009 Article 292 for two-seater racing cars required a securely mounted dry-chemical fire extinguisher of at least 1 kg, scatter-shield protection where clutch or flywheel failure could endanger the driver, roll-over protection, quick-release safety belt attached to the chassis structure, adequate firewall/floor protection, fuel/oil/water tank isolation from the cockpit, fuel-tank venting, fuel bulkhead isolation, and fully enclosed batteries.
applicability: Article-level period safety benchmark; not automatically proof of Group 4 GT40 compliance.

notes: Use as a detailed period safety checklist alongside current Appendix K and event rules; installed equipment, mounting, dates, condition, and event requirements remain open.

fact: S019 Appendix K Article 5.15.2 requires shoulder straps to run rearward and downward without exceeding 45 degrees to horizontal from the upper backrest edge, recommends not exceeding 10 degrees, says lap and crotch straps must pass through the seat rather than over the sides, and says lap straps must hold the pelvic region rather than the abdomen while avoiding chafing on sharp edges.
applicability: Current historic harness routing and body-contact benchmark.
label: verified
notes: Use for safety evidence capture and inspection only; installed seat, belt, anchor, FHR, and event applicability remain unverified.

fact: S019 Appendix K Article 5.15.2 says that when shoulder or crotch straps cannot use series anchorage points, new anchorage points must be installed on the shell or chassis; shoulder strap anchors should be as near as possible to the rear-wheel centreline, and listed reinforcement/anchorage requirements include transverse tube size/material, M12 8.8 or 7/16 UNF bolt options, 1470 daN anchorage load or 720 daN crotch-strap load, and steel reinforcement plates of at least 40 cm2 by 3 mm.
label: verified
notes: Do not approve existing anchors from appearance; inspect material, geometry, load path, reinforcement, fasteners, seat pass-throughs, and manufacturer instructions before event use.

fact: S019 Appendix K Article 5.21 sets seat-replacement rules for listed GTS, CT, GT, and T categories from Period F onward, including period-specification or FIA-homologated complete seat-system requirements depending on category; applicability to a GT40-style car depends on its HTP/category/event classification.
applicability: Current historic seat replacement classification boundary.
label: verified
notes: Do not apply Article 5.21 generically to the project car; first verify FIA period/category, seat identity, mounting, original-seat status, and event requirements.

fact: S019 Appendix K Article 5.10.1 requires two rear-view mirrors for racing with minimum total glass area of 90 cm2.
label: verified
notes: Installed mirror count, glass area, mounting, driver visibility, vibration, and event applicability remain unverified.

fact: S019 Appendix K Articles 5.11.1 and 5.14.1 require front-lamp glass to be taped or covered in closed-circuit races and require cars originally equipped with rear brake lights to have them working at race start.

label: verified
notes: Use as an inspection prompt only; installed lamp material, tape/cover method, brake-light switch, wiring, fuse, bulb/LED output, and event rules remain unverified.

fact: S019 Appendix K Article 5.14.2 requires a working rear-facing red warning light at race start that is clearly visible from the rear, solidly attached, driver-switchable, less than 10 cm from the car centreline, 20-40 cm2 in area, and at least 21 W incandescent or equivalent LED output; full-width bodywork may alternatively use two lights at equal distance from the centreline.
label: verified
notes: Installed warning-light location, area, output, switch, wiring, attachment, visibility, and full-width-bodywork applicability remain unverified.
